channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: VIDEO: Moment security operatives rescued abducted Oyo schoolchildren, teachers
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Watch the emotional moment security operatives rescued abducted Oyo schoolchildren and teachers after 50 days. Learn more about the successful rescue opera
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-moment-security-operatives-rescued-abducted-oyo-schoolchildren-teachers/

item.title: Highlights: In-control India leave England questioning on day two
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Highlights of day two of the Women's Test between England and India.

item.title: Millions flee as Typhoon Bavi hits China, causes travel disruption
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Typhoon Bavi makes landfall in eastern China, forcing nearly two million evacuations and causing widespread travel disruption. Get the latest updates on th
Read More: https://punchng.com/millions-flee-as-typhoon-bavi-hits-china-causes-travel-disruption/

item.title: Noskova recovers to win maiden Wimbledon title after six Championship points
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Linda Noskova sealed her maiden Grand Slam title as she became the third Czech women's Wimbledon champion in four years with victory over compatriot Karolina Muchova on Saturday - but only after overcoming a major wobble.

item.title: 'Naive' England toil in Women's Test as India dominate at Lord's
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England are facing an almighty battle to avoid defeat in the first-ever Women's Test at Lord's after India dominated day two of the four-day fixture.

item.title: Former Chelsea chairman Ken Bates dies at 94
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Former Chelsea chairman Ken Bates has died at 94. Learn about his pivotal role in Chelsea’s history, from buying the club for £1 to selling it to Abramovic
Read More: https://punchng.com/former-chelsea-chairman-ken-bates-dies-at-94/

item.title: Remi Tinubu defends akara comment, appeals to Davido, others to fund poor
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
First Lady Remi Tinubu defends her akara comment, urging Davido and other entertainers to fund the poor through charitable foundations.

item.title: Norway's 'Viking Row' has found its way to golf! Hovland laps up support in Scotland
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Norway's 'Viking Row' has made its way to the world of golf, where fans supported Viktor Hovland at the Scottish Open.

item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: PDP Taraba assistant secretary resigns, joins APC after Kefas appointment
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The State Assistant Secretary of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) in Taraba State, James Chimin, has resigned from the party and declared his support for the All Progressives Congress (APC) after accepting an appointment as Senior Special Assistant (SSA) to Governor Agbu Kefas. Speaking with journ

item.title: Calabar flood victims count losses in millions after devastating downpour
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Flash floods in Calabar claimed two lives, displaced families and destroyed property worth millions as residents appealed for urgent government assistance. The post Calabar flood victims count losses in millions after devastating downpour appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: China's second typhoon in a week makes landfall
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Nearly two million people are evacuated from eastern Zhejiang province, with the city of Wenzhou close to the path of the storm.

item.title: Flamingos Edge Benin To Book Place At 2026 U-17 Women’s World Cup
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigeria’s Flamingos have qualified for the 2026 FIFA U-17 Women’s World Cup after securing a thrilling 5-3 victory over Benin Republic at the Stade de Kégué in Lomé on Saturday. Akeem Busari’s side advanced 8-5 on aggregate, having won the first leg of the final qualifying round 3-2 in Ikenne. The F

item.title: ]JUST IN] Nigeria’s Flamingos Qualify For U-17 Women’s W/Cup After Victory Over Benin Republic
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: With a 3-5 win on Saturday, Nigeria picked one of the tickets for the competition billed for later this year on an 8-5 aggregate. The post ]JUST IN] Nigeria’s Flamingos Qualify For U-17 Women’s W/Cup After Victory Over Benin Republic appeared first on Channels Television.

Isaacs Kekemeke, has commended President Bola Tinubu and the nation’s security agencies for the successful rescue of pupils and teachers abducted in Oyo State. Kekemeke’s p

item.title: Fight bandits, not critics, coalition tells Katsina gov’t
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Coalition of Northern Groups (CNG), Katsina State chapter, has condemned the arrest and continued detention of public affairs analyst, Dr. Bashir Kurfi, urging the Katsina State Government to prioritise the fight against banditry rather than clamp down on critics. The post AfriSAFE lauds Ghana president’s leadership on post-flood sanitation drive appeared first o

item.title: Transfers: Wolves’ stance revealed as Trabzonspor continue pursuit of Arokodare
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Wolves have made their position on Super Eagles striker Tolu Arokodare clear amid growing interest from Turkish side Trabzonspor and several other European clubs, Soccernet.ng reports. Arokodare's future has been the subject of speculation following Wolves' relegation from the Premier League to the

item.title: Smart glasses without a camera? Even Realities bets productivity beats recording everyone
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: The glasses are targeted at people who might be constantly in meetings, giving presentations, and traveling to countries where different languages are spoken.

item.title: Billy Loughnane steers Comanche Brave to July Cup glory
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Billy Loughnane’s exceptional season continued at Newmarket on Saturday as he steered Irish raider Comanche Brave to a brilliant sprinting success in the Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai July Cup Stakes.

item.title: Arsenal Considering Re-signing Former Gunners Star
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Arsenal are interested in re-signing Dinos Mavropanos after the Greece international was relegated with West Ham. Mavropanos, 28, is wanted by several clubs and could be sold as part of a fire sale at the London Stadium. The defender is no stranger to the Gunners, who bought him from Greek club PAS

item.title: Lagos police arrest five suspected cultists terrorising Apapa, Ijora areas
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Operatives of the Lagos State Police Command have arrested five suspected cultists alleged to be terrorising residents of Apapa, Ijora and Badia areas of the state. The post Lagos police arrest five suspected cultists terrorising Apapa, Ijora areas appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.description: The National Association of Nigerian Students (NANS) will on Tuesday, July 14, inaugurate the new leadership of the student body led by National President, Comrade Babatunde Akinteye. The post NANS to inaugurate national exco Tuesday appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Wembanyama Signs New $252M Lonf-Term Deal With Spurs
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Wembanyama has signed a long-term deal with the San Antonio Spurs. The 7ft 4in France centre’s deal, according to ESPN, is for five years and worth $252m (£188m), which is the NBA’s maximum for a rookie scale player extension. The 22-year-old reportedly has an option in the fifth season

item.description: The European Parliament has asked the Nigerian government to take decisive action against insecurity in the country, especially in the Middle Belt region. This came amid growing concerns over attacks on Christian communities in the region. No fewer than 501 lawmakers voted in support of a resolution

item.title: Gov Aiyedatiwa Meets Dangote To Advance Olokola FTZ Project
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Governor of Ondo State, Lucky Aiyedatiwa, has held meet with the Founder and President of the Dangote Group, Aliko Dangote, in Lagos, as part of ongoing efforts to advance the development of the Olokola Free Trade Zone (OKFTZ). The post Gov Aiyedatiwa Meets Dangote To Advance Olokola FTZ Project app

item.title: SIMS kicks off online shopping promo with up to 65% off for Lagos customers
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: SIMS Nigeria has launched its Online Shopping Spree Promo, offering Lagos customers discounts of up to 65 per cent on a wide range of original electronics and home appliances. The online promotion, which runs from July 6 until August 8, 2026, is available exclusively through the company’s e-co

item.title: Colourful, controversial and significant - ex-Chelsea and Leeds owner Bates dies
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Former Chelsea and Leeds owner Ken Bates, who has died aged 94, was a colourful and controversial figure - but also a significant one for English football, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: Transfer: Super Falcons midfielder joins Inter Women on two-year deal
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Inter Women have announced the signing of Super Falcons midfielder Toni Payne, DAILY POST reports. Payne made the switch from English Women’s Super League, WSL, club Everton Ladies. The 31-year-old penned a contract with the Nerazzurri until  30 June, 2028. The former United States o

item.title: Ooni commends Tinubu, security agencies for rescue of Oyo schoolchildren, teachers
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Enitan Ogunwusi, has commended President Bola Tinubu, the Oyo State Government and Nigeria’s security agencies for the successful rescue of abducted Oriire schoolchildren and teachers. In a statement issued on Saturday by the Director of Media and Public

item.title: Cuba suffers second nationwide blackout in five days
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Cuba suffered its second nationwide blackout in five days on Friday as twin energy and economic crises, worsened by a six-month US fuel blockade, continue to cripple the country's electricity grid. "The pressure is increasing," explains our journalist Shirli Sitbon.

item.title: We will support Oriire victims until full recovery, says Makinde
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: He wrote: "Earlier today, we visited the victims of the Oriire LGA kidnapping incident where they are receiving treatment in Ibadan. We will continue to provide the necessary support needed for their full recovery and reunite them with their families."
